name: Khao Soi Soup
method: Prepare to delight your taste buds with this flavorful khao soi recipe that harmoniously blends Thai spices, tender chicken, and creamy coconut milk. The dish is elegantly served over egg noodles, resulting in a culinary masterpiece that will make your efforts in the kitchen truly worthwhile.
Chopped pickled mustard greens - 1 ounce
Chopped pickled mustard greens - 1 ounce
In a small bowl, carefully add the dried red chilies to the boiling hot water and soak them for 15-20 minutes.
In a small bowl, carefully add the dried red chilies to the boiling hot water and soak them for 15-20 minutes.
Heat coconut oil in a Dutch oven over medium-low heat, then add 1 (For a less spicy option) or 2 tablespoons (For a spicier option) of khao soi paste (Step 3).
Heat coconut oil in a Dutch oven over medium-low heat, then add 1 (For a less spicy option) or 2 tablespoons (For a spicier option) of khao soi paste (Step 3).
Add curry, turmeric and pepper into the Dutch oven and fry until golden brown.
Add curry, turmeric and pepper into the Dutch oven and fry until golden brown.
Add 3 cups of coconut milk into the Dutch oven. You may add 1 more cup of coconut milk (meaning 4 cups in total) if the broth gets too thick or if you want it to be less spicy. Let the broth boil
Add 3 cups of coconut milk into the Dutch oven. You may add 1 more cup of coconut milk (meaning 4 cups in total) if the broth gets too thick or if you want it to be less spicy. Let the broth boil
Add the chicken into the Dutch oven and partially cover it with a heavy lid. Let it simmer for 20 to 25 minutes
Add the chicken into the Dutch oven and partially cover it with a heavy lid. Let it simmer for 20 to 25 minutes
Transfer half of the chicken to a pan and let it cool for about 3 to 4 minutes. Debone and tear the chicken with the aid of forks and/or tongs. When done, return to the broth
Transfer half of the chicken to a pan and let it cool for about 3 to 4 minutes. Debone and tear the chicken with the aid of forks and/or tongs. When done, return to the broth
Add fish sauce and palm sugar to the broth. Let it continue simmering on low heat.
Add fish sauce and palm sugar to the broth. Let it continue simmering on low heat.
In a separate pot, cook the wide egg noodles according to package directions. When done, immediately rinse in cold water for a few minutes and separate them to prevent them from sticking together.
In a separate pot, cook the wide egg noodles according to package directions. When done, immediately rinse in cold water for a few minutes and separate them to prevent them from sticking together.
Add a quart of cooking oil in a separate cast iron skillet on medium heat, then deep fry the thin egg noodles until it turns caramel brown.
Add a quart of cooking oil in a separate cast iron skillet on medium heat, then deep fry the thin egg noodles until it turns caramel brown.
Serve all of the topping ingredients on individual small plates
Serve all of the topping ingredients on individual small plates
Transfer the egg noodles (Step 10) and soup (Step 9) to a bowl. Top it with the crispy noodles (Step 12) and the topping ingredients (Step 13) as desired!
Transfer the egg noodles (Step 10) and soup (Step 9) to a bowl. Top it with the crispy noodles (Step 12) and the topping ingredients (Step 13) as desired!
